title: First Impressions & Public Website
objective: Validate that the public website creates a powerful, immediate, and professional first impression that accurately represents Dr. Kavinda Gunasekara.
personaFocus: Alex (First-Time Visitor) · Maria (Professional Evaluator) · James (Mobile Visitor)

items.title: First Page Load Creates a Strong Impression
items.userStory: As a first-time visitor, I want the website to load quickly and look immediately impressive, so that I trust the person behind it is serious and professional.
items.preconditions: Visiting the website for the first time (no cached data).
steps: Open a fresh browser window (no cache, no extensions)
Navigate to the main website URL
Observe the initial load experience
criteria: The website fully loads within **5 seconds** on a standard broadband connection
The hero section is visually striking and communicates Dr. Kavinda's identity within 3 seconds
Dr. Kavinda's name, title ("Project Management Specialist / FAO"), and a professional photo or visual are visible immediately
No broken images, missing fonts, or layout shifts after load
The overall design feels premium, modern, and trustworthy

items.title: Hero Section Communicates Value Clearly
items.userStory: As a professional evaluator, I want to immediately understand who this person is and what makes them exceptional, so that I can decide within 10 seconds whether to explore further.
steps: Land on the homepage
Read only what is visible without scrolling (above the fold)
criteria: The headline clearly states Dr. Kavinda's name and professional role
A subtitle or tagline communicates key credentials (e.g. FAO, 18+ years, international scope)
At least one clear Call-to-Action button is visible (e.g. "View My Work", "Download CV")
The visual design is consistent with the expected quality of a senior international expert

items.title: Navigation Is Intuitive and Complete
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to navigate the site effortlessly and find any section I'm looking for, so that I don't get frustrated or leave.
steps: Examine the main navigation bar
Click each navigation item
Verify all sections are accessible
criteria: Navigation is clearly visible and readable on desktop
All major sections are represented: Projects, Research, Publications, Timeline, Network, About
Clicking each nav item scrolls to or navigates to the correct section
The active section is visually highlighted in the nav as the user scrolls
Navigation is accessible on a fresh page load without JavaScript errors

items.title: The 3D Globe Creates a "Wow" Moment
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to see a visually engaging representation of Kavinda's global reach, so that I immediately understand the international scale of his work.
steps: Scroll to the globe / world map section
Interact with the globe (drag, scroll, click)
Observe country markers and data
criteria: The 3D globe renders visually and is interactive
The globe can be rotated by dragging with the mouse
Country markers or highlights are visible representing the 20+ countries
The globe does not freeze, crash, or cause significant performance degradation
The visual makes a powerful statement about the breadth of Dr. Kavinda's geographic reach

items.title: Projects Section Showcases Work Professionally
items.userStory: As a researcher or evaluator, I want to browse Kavinda's projects and understand the depth and scale of his work, so that I can assess his expertise.
steps: Scroll to the Projects section
Browse the 10 project cards
Click on 2–3 different projects to view details
criteria: All 10 projects are displayed with a title, description, and visual
Each project card gives enough information to understand the project's nature and context
Clicking a project opens a detail page with full content (body text, images, metadata)
The project detail page feels complete and professional — not placeholder or thin
Navigation back to the homepage is intuitive after viewing a project detail

items.title: Publications Section Establishes Academic Credibility
items.userStory: As a fellow researcher, I want to see Dr. Kavinda's full publication list with proper academic citations and DOI links, so that I can access and cite his work.
steps: Navigate to the Publications section
Browse the 22 publications
Click a DOI link on any publication
criteria: All 22 publications are listed and readable
Each publication shows: title, authors, journal name, year, and citation count
DOI links open the correct external paper in a new browser tab
The total citation count (306+) and h-index (9) are prominently displayed
The presentation feels academically rigorous — not informal or incomplete

items.title: Timeline Tells a Compelling Career Story
items.userStory: As a professional evaluator, I want to see a visual timeline of Dr. Kavinda's career milestones, so that I can quickly understand the trajectory and depth of his experience.
steps: Scroll to the Journey / Timeline section
Read through the 14 milestones
Click on a milestone to explore its related content
criteria: All 14 milestones are visible and chronologically ordered (Sep 2016 onwards)
Each milestone shows: date, title, subtitle, and contextual information
Visual badges and imagery make the timeline scannable and engaging
Clicking a milestone opens related content or a side panel with more detail
The timeline communicates a clear narrative of growth and international impact

items.title: Research Areas Are Clearly Explained
items.userStory: As a researcher, I want to understand Dr. Kavinda's specific research domains so that I can assess if our work overlaps and collaboration would be valuable.
steps: Navigate to the Research section
Read all 6 research area descriptions
Click on one to see its detail page
criteria: All 6 research areas are listed with titles and descriptions
Each research area is clearly explained in plain language
Related publications or projects are linked from each research area
The research area detail page feels informative and complete

items.title: Network Visualization Shows Professional Connections
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to see how Dr. Kavinda's work, projects, and expertise are interconnected, so that I understand the depth of his professional network and knowledge graph.
steps: Navigate to the Network / Knowledge Graph section
Click on several nodes
Explore connections between nodes
criteria: The network diagram loads and is interactive (clickable nodes)
Clicking a node reveals its label, description, and related content
The visualization is not overwhelming — it's legible and navigable
Links between nodes are visually clear
The experience conveys sophistication and depth of expertise

items.title: About Section Humanizes the Professional
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to read a personal and professional bio so that I understand who Dr. Kavinda is beyond his credentials.
steps: Navigate to the About section
Read the biography and stats
Check all information for accuracy
criteria: Bio text is compelling, accurate, and reads naturally
Key stats are prominently displayed: 18+ years experience, 20+ countries, 306+ citations, h-index 9
Professional photo or visual is present and loads correctly
The section feels personal and trustworthy — not robotic or generic

items.title: CV Download Works Correctly
items.userStory: As an HR professional, I want to download Dr. Kavinda's CV so that I can review it offline and share it with colleagues.
steps: Find the "Download CV" button or link (on hero section or about section)
Click it
criteria: A clearly labeled CV download button or link exists on the page
Clicking it downloads `Kavinda_Gunasekara_CV.pdf` immediately or opens it in a new tab
The PDF opens correctly and contains complete, accurate, up-to-date information
The file name is professional (not "document.pdf" or "file1.pdf")

items.title: Related Content Cross-Navigation Keeps Visitors Engaged
items.userStory: As a researcher, I want to explore related content between projects, articles, and publications without losing my place, so that I can follow threads of interest naturally.
steps: Open a project detail page
Click a related article or publication in the "Related Content" section
Navigate back and try another related item
criteria: A "Related Content" section exists on project, article, blog, and publication detail pages
Clicking a related item opens a side panel or navigates to the item — without losing context
The back navigation works logically (user can return to where they started)
Related items are genuinely relevant (not random)
This feature encourages deeper exploration of the site

items.title: Search Finds the Right Content Quickly
items.userStory: As a visitor looking for specific content, I want to search the site and get relevant results quickly, so that I don't have to manually browse every section.
steps: Use the search feature on the public site
Search for "FAO" — observe results
Search for a publication title keyword — observe results
Search for something that doesn't exist — observe results
criteria: Search is easily discoverable on the public site
Results appear within 2 seconds of submitting a query
Search returns relevant content from projects, publications, articles, and blogs
Results clearly show the content type (Project / Publication / Article / Blog)
An empty state is shown gracefully for no results (not an error)

title: Mobile Experience
objective: Validate that the platform delivers an excellent experience on mobile and tablet devices.

items.title: Homepage Is Fully Functional on Mobile (iPhone)
items.userStory: As a mobile visitor, I want the website to look great and work perfectly on my phone, so that I don't have to pinch, zoom, or scroll horizontally to read anything.
steps: Open the website on an iPhone (or Chrome DevTools at 375px viewport)
Scroll through the full homepage
Try clicking navigation, project cards, and the floating AI button
criteria: Zero horizontal overflow (no content wider than the screen)
All text is readable without zooming
Navigation collapses to a hamburger menu or similar mobile-friendly pattern
All project, article, and publication cards stack vertically and are tappable
The floating AI button is visible and accessible on mobile
The 3D globe renders without crashing (or gracefully degrades on low-end devices)
CTA buttons have appropriate tap target sizes (minimum 44x44px)

items.title: Project Detail Page Reads Correctly on Mobile
items.userStory: As a mobile visitor, I want to read a full project description without any formatting issues.
steps: On mobile, tap any project card
Read the project detail page in full
criteria: Title, body text, images, and metadata all display correctly on mobile
Images do not overflow the screen
Related content section is accessible and usable on mobile
Back navigation is accessible

items.title: Tablet Experience Is Polished (iPad)
items.userStory: As a professional visiting on a tablet, I want a layout that makes good use of the larger screen while still being touch-friendly.
steps: Open the website on an iPad (or 768px viewport)
Browse the full homepage
criteria: Cards use a 2-column or 3-column grid layout appropriate for the tablet screen width
Touch targets are large enough for comfortable tapping
No desktop-only hover states break the experience
Typography scales appropriately — not too large, not too small

items.title: Sharing a Link From Mobile Works
items.userStory: As a mobile visitor, I want to copy and share the URL of a specific project or publication so that I can send it to a colleague.
steps: Navigate to a project detail page on mobile
Copy the URL from the browser address bar
Paste it in a new tab or message
criteria: Each content page has a unique, shareable URL (e.g. /project/sustainable-agriculture)
Opening a shared URL goes directly to the correct content — not just the homepage
The URL is clean and human-readable (not a random hash)

title: Public AI Assistant (Floating Widget)
objective: Validate that the public-facing AI assistant is genuinely helpful, accurate, and enhances the visitor experience.

items.title: The Floating AI Widget Is Visible and Inviting
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to notice the AI assistant option without being distracted by it, so that I can choose to engage with it when I need help.
steps: Load the homepage
Notice the floating AI button
criteria: A floating chat/AI button is visible on the homepage (typically bottom-right)
The button is visually distinct and recognizable as an AI chat trigger
The button does not obscure important page content
On mobile, the button is appropriately sized and positioned

items.title: AI Answers "Who Is Kavinda?" Correctly
items.userStory: As a professional evaluator, I want to ask the AI a simple question about Kavinda and receive an accurate, professional summary, so that I can quickly understand his profile.
steps: Open the floating AI widget
Type: "Who is Dr. Kavinda Gunasekara?"
Read the response
criteria: The AI responds within 10 seconds
The response accurately describes Dr. Kavinda's role (FAO Project Management Specialist)
Key credentials are mentioned: years of experience, countries, academic metrics
The response is professional in tone — not robotic or generic
No incorrect information is presented (no hallucinated details)

items.title: AI Can Discuss Specific Publications
items.userStory: As a researcher, I want to ask the AI about specific publications so that I can quickly assess their relevance without reading everything manually.
steps: Open the floating AI widget
Type: "What publications does Kavinda have on food security?"
Read the response
Follow up: "Can you tell me more about the most cited one?"
criteria: The AI references actual publications from the site (not invented ones)
The response includes titles and relevant context
The follow-up question is understood in context of the first question (multi-turn conversation works)
The AI suggests visiting the publications section for the full list

items.title: AI Handles Questions About Projects and Countries
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to ask specific questions about Kavinda's projects and get useful answers.
steps: Ask: "What countries has Kavinda worked in?"
Ask: "Tell me about his work in Sri Lanka"
Ask: "What is his most recent project?"
criteria: All three questions receive relevant, data-driven responses
The country information is sourced from the actual 20 countries in the database
Responses are conversational and helpful — not just raw data dumps
No wrong country is cited as one Kavinda has worked in

items.title: Voice Input Works for Hands-Free Use
items.userStory: As a mobile visitor, I want to speak my question instead of typing it, so that I can interact with the AI while on the go.
steps: Open the AI widget on mobile
Tap the microphone button
Speak a question: "What research has Kavinda done on sustainable agriculture?"
Observe transcription and response
criteria: A microphone button is visible in the chat interface
Tapping the microphone starts recording (visual indicator shown)
The spoken question is transcribed and appears in the input field
The transcription is accurate enough to be understood by the AI
The AI responds to the transcribed question correctly

items.title: AI Gracefully Handles Out-of-Scope Questions
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want the AI to handle unexpected or off-topic questions gracefully, so that I don't encounter a confusing error.
steps: Ask: "What's the weather like in Colombo today?"
Ask: "Help me write a poem"
Ask: "What's the stock price of Apple?"
criteria: The AI politely acknowledges it cannot help with off-topic requests
The AI redirects the conversation to what it can help with (Kavinda's work)
No error messages or crashes occur
The response remains professional and on-brand

title: Admin Dashboard & Content Management
objective: Validate that Kavinda can efficiently log in, manage all content types, and publish changes without requiring technical support.

items.title: Login Is Secure and Simple
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to log in quickly and securely, so that I can access my admin dashboard without frustration.
steps: Navigate to the admin login page
Enter admin credentials
Access the dashboard
criteria: The login page is clearly branded and easy to find
Login succeeds with correct credentials within 2 seconds
An incorrect password shows a clear error without revealing which field is wrong
After login, the admin dashboard is shown immediately
The session persists for at least 7 days without requiring re-login

items.title: Admin Dashboard Gives Immediate Overview
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to see a dashboard summary when I log in, so that I know the current state of my content at a glance.
steps: Log in to the admin panel
View the dashboard home tab
criteria: Content counts are clearly displayed (projects, articles, blogs, publications, etc.)
The dashboard is visually clean and easy to scan
Navigation to all content sections is one click away
Any unread notifications or alerts are visible prominently

items.title: Creating a New Project Is Straightforward
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to create a new project entry without technical knowledge, so that I can publish my work independently.
steps: Navigate to the Projects section in admin
Click "Create New Project"
Fill in: title, body text (with rich formatting), status, cover image
Save and then view the public site
criteria: The "Create" button is clearly visible and labeled
The creation form includes all necessary fields: title, body, excerpt, status, cover image, tags
The rich text editor (body) allows formatting: bold, headings, lists, links, images
The saved project appears in the admin list immediately
Publishing the project makes it appear on the public site within 30 seconds
The public page looks professional and complete

items.title: Editing Existing Content Is Quick and Error-Free
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to edit any piece of content and have the change reflected on the public site immediately, so that I can keep my portfolio up-to-date.
steps: Find an existing project (e.g. Project 1)
Click Edit
Change the title
Save
View the public site
criteria: Edit buttons are clearly visible on each content item
The edit form pre-populates with the existing content — no starting from scratch
Saving is a single click
The change is reflected on the public site within 30 seconds
No data is lost during the edit (other fields remain unchanged)

items.title: Draft vs Published Status Controls Visibility
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to save content as a draft and only publish it when I'm ready, so that unfinished work never appears on the public site.
steps: Create a new article with status "Draft"
Check the public site — verify the article is not visible
Change the status to "Published"
Check the public site — verify the article is now visible
criteria: Draft and Published status options are clearly labeled
Draft content is invisible on the public site
Published content is visible on the public site
Switching between draft and published takes effect immediately
It's clear in the admin list which items are drafts vs. published

items.title: Uploading a New Image Is Simple
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to upload images easily and use them across my content, so that my portfolio stays visually rich.
steps: Navigate to the Media section in admin
Upload a new JPEG image
Set alt text and a caption
Use the media library to insert the image into a project body
criteria: Uploading a file is drag-and-drop or a simple file browser click
The image appears in the media library within 5 seconds of upload
Alt text and caption can be set without confusion
The image can be selected from the media library when editing content
The image loads correctly on the public site

items.title: Uploading and Cropping an Image Works Correctly
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to crop uploaded images to ensure they look perfect as cover images, so that my portfolio looks polished.
steps: Upload a new image to the media library
Open the crop tool
Define a crop area
Apply the crop
criteria: A crop tool is available on uploaded images
The crop area can be adjusted freely with visible handles
Applying the crop saves a new cropped version
The cropped version is the one used as the card thumbnail by default
The original image is preserved alongside the cropped version

items.title: My Workflow Editor Allows Natural Writing
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want a Notion-like writing experience for editing my content, so that writing and updating feels natural and not technical.
steps: Navigate to the "My Workflow" tab in admin
Browse the file tree (all content types listed)
Click on a project to open it
Edit the title
Use the rich text toolbar to add a bold heading and a bullet list
Wait 30 seconds without manually saving
criteria: The file tree clearly shows all content types and how many items exist
Clicking any item opens it in the editor immediately
The editor feels like a modern writing tool (Notion-style, not a form)
The toolbar includes: Bold, Italic, Headings, Lists, Links, Images, Code blocks
Auto-save triggers after 30 seconds (a visual indicator confirms it saved)
No data is lost during auto-save

items.title: Timeline Milestone Can Be Added With Visual Positioning
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to add a new career milestone to the timeline and control how it appears visually, so that my career story is told accurately.
steps: Navigate to the Timeline section in admin
Create a new milestone (title, date, subtitle, description, image URL)
Set the visual positioning fields (axis, line height)
Add 2 tags and 1 related content item
Save and check the public timeline
criteria: The create milestone form is clear and all fields are labeled
Visual positioning fields can be set with numeric values
Tags can be added by typing
Related content can be searched and linked
The saved milestone appears on the public timeline in the correct position

items.title: Site Settings Can Be Updated Without a Developer
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to update my bio, hero text, and site metadata without needing a developer, so that I maintain full control of my platform.
steps: Navigate to the Settings section in admin
Update the hero headline text
Update the About bio text
Save and check the public site
criteria: Settings are clearly organized into logical sections (Hero, About, Navigation, Footer, Meta, AI)
Updating a text setting is a simple field-edit-and-save flow
Changes appear on the public site within 30 seconds of saving
No technical knowledge is required to understand what each setting controls
Accidentally mis-typing in one field does not break other settings

items.title: Related Content Linking Is Intuitive
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to link my projects, articles, and publications together so that visitors can explore my interconnected body of work.
steps: Open the edit form for any project
In the Related Content section, search for and add an article and a publication
Reorder the related items
Save and check the public project detail page
criteria: The related content picker has a search bar and type filter
Selecting items is a single click — no complex configuration needed
Items can be reordered by dragging
Items can be removed with a single click
The saved related content appears correctly on the public site

items.title: Admin Password Can Be Changed Securely
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to change my admin password securely so that I can maintain the security of my platform.
steps: Navigate to the password change section in admin settings
Enter current password, new password, and confirm new password
Submit
criteria: The password change form requires the current password (no unauthorized changes)
A success confirmation is shown when the change succeeds
The old password no longer works after the change
The new password works immediately for the next login
The process works within the admin interface — no developer needed

title: Lara Super Agent: Intelligence & Automation
objective: Validate that Lara is a genuinely intelligent, safe, and powerful AI assistant that can autonomously manage the platform with proper oversight.

items.title: Lara Understands the Full Platform Context
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want Lara to know everything about my platform — all my content, settings, and structure — so that I can ask it questions and delegate tasks without having to explain context every time.
steps: Open the Lara AI Assistant tab
Ask: "Give me a summary of everything on my site right now"
Ask: "How many publications do I have and what's my total citation count?"
criteria: Lara responds with an accurate summary of the platform's content
The publication count (22) and citation count (306+) are correct
Lara's knowledge reflects the current state of the database — not outdated data
The response is intelligently formatted and easy to read
Response time is acceptable (under 30 seconds for a full site summary)

items.title: Lara Can Update Content and Asks for Approval First
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want Lara to be able to update my content when I ask it to, but I want to review and approve every change before it goes live, so that I always stay in control.
steps: Ask Lara: "Update the title of my first project to 'Sustainable Food Systems Initiative'"
Observe the authorization request that appears
Review the details of the proposed change
Click Authorize
criteria: Lara does NOT make the change immediately — it presents it for approval first
The authorization card clearly shows: what will be changed, from what, to what
The approval button is clearly labeled and a single click
After approval, the project title is updated in the database
The change is visible on the public site within 30 seconds
A cancel option is available if the proposed change is not desired

items.title: Lara Can Create New Content Entries
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to tell Lara to create a new blog post or project and have it draft the content for me, so that I can publish faster.
steps: Ask Lara: "Create a new draft blog post about my experience presenting at the FAO Regional Conference"
Review the authorization request for the new blog creation
Approve it
criteria: Lara proposes a complete blog post with title, body, and appropriate metadata
The content is relevant, professional, and written in a style consistent with Dr. Kavinda's voice
An authorization request is shown before the post is created
After approval, the draft appears in the admin blog list
The content can then be edited manually before publishing

items.title: Authorize All Mode Allows Efficient Bulk Operations
items.userStory: As the site owner, when I have a lot of updates to make, I want to tell Lara to "just do everything" and trust it to execute, so that I can get large updates done efficiently.
steps: Give Lara a multi-step task (e.g. "Update the excerpt of all 5 articles to be more concise")
Review the multiple authorization requests that appear
Click "Authorize All"
criteria: Multiple authorization requests are presented together when a multi-step task is proposed
"Authorize All" is a clearly visible button
Clicking "Authorize All" executes all pending actions in sequence
Each action is logged in the audit trail
The system switches to "auto mode" after Authorize All for the rest of the session

items.title: Lara Remembers Conversation Context
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want Lara to remember what we've talked about in our conversation, so that I can have a natural multi-turn dialogue without repeating context.
steps: Ask Lara: "Which of my projects has the least content?"
Follow up: "Add a paragraph to it about the key outcomes of the project"
Follow up again: "Now generate a cover image for it"
criteria: Each follow-up correctly builds on the previous message
Lara does not ask "which project?" after step 1 established it
All three requests are fulfilled in a coherent conversation thread
No context is lost during the conversation

items.title: Background Tasks Run Without Blocking the Interface
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to ask Lara to do a large task and then navigate away, knowing that Lara will complete the task in the background and notify me when it's done.
steps: Ask Lara: "Run a full site scan and give me a complete summary"
Navigate to a different admin tab while it runs
Wait for the notification
criteria: Lara starts the task and shows a visual indicator that it is running
The admin interface remains fully usable while the task runs
A desktop notification appears when the task is complete (if browser notifications are permitted)
The task result is available in the chat when returning to the Lara tab
No timeout or error occurs for tasks that take longer than 30 seconds

items.title: Lara Can Scan the Site and Report Changes
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want Lara to periodically scan my site and tell me if anything has changed or gone missing, so that I catch errors before my visitors do.
steps: Navigate to the Lara Scan tab
Click "Run Scan Now"
View the scan report
Check the diff (changes since last scan)
criteria: The scan completes within 60 seconds
The report shows totals for all content types (projects, articles, publications, etc.)
If any content has changed since the last scan, the diff is clearly highlighted
The diff distinguishes between: Added, Changed, and Removed items
A notification is created in the notification inbox on scan completion

items.title: Lara's Engine Configuration Is Accessible and Clear
items.userStory: As an advanced user, I want to be able to see which AI engines Lara is using and configure them if needed, so that I understand what is powering my AI assistant.
steps: Check Lara's status or engine configuration screen
View the list of 10 engines
View the available models for one engine
criteria: A status or settings page for Lara's engines is accessible in the admin
All 10 engines are listed: Orchestrator, Reasoning, Code, Vision, Memory, Speech, Safety, Image, Perception, Omni
Each engine shows its current status (active/inactive) and current model
The model can be changed from a list of available options
A "Validate" button confirms whether the engine's API connection is working

title: Image Generation & Media Intelligence
objective: Validate that Lara can generate professional images, manage them intelligently, and apply them to content with minimal manual effort.

items.title: Lara Generates a High-Quality Image From a Description
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to tell Lara what kind of image I need and have it generate a professional image for me, so that I don't have to source stock photos.
steps: Ask Lara: "Generate a professional image of a sustainable farming landscape in Southeast Asia at golden hour"
Wait for the image to be generated
View the generated image in the chat
criteria: Lara confirms the image generation task has started
The image is generated within 60 seconds
The generated image appears in the chat as a visible image — not a broken link or raw code
The image quality is professional and matches the description provided
The image is persisted — refreshing the page does not lose the image

items.title: Generated Image Can Be Immediately Applied as Content Cover
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to instantly use a generated image as the cover photo for one of my content pieces, so that I can update my portfolio visuals without leaving the chat.
steps: Generate an image (UAT-F-001)
Tell Lara: "Use this image as the cover of my latest blog post"
Approve the authorization
Check the blog post on the public site
criteria: Lara understands "this image" refers to the most recently generated image
An authorization request appears showing: action = update cover image, target = latest blog
After approval, the blog's cover image is updated in the database
The new cover image appears on the public blog card within 30 seconds

items.title: Multiple Images Can Be Generated in One Session
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to generate several images in one session and have them all available for use, so that I can build a set of visuals for a new content batch.
steps: Ask for Image 1: "Generate a professional headshot-style background for a project card"
Ask for Image 2: "Generate an abstract visualization of data networks"
Ask for Image 3: "Generate a photorealistic image of the FAO headquarters in Rome"
View all 3 images in the chat
criteria: All 3 images are generated without errors
Each image is independently accessible via its own persistent URL
The images are visually distinct and match their respective prompts
The chat history clearly shows all 3 images without overlap or confusion

items.title: Chat History Does Not Show Raw Base64 Image Data
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want the chat interface to remain clean and readable even after generating images, so that the conversation is still useful for follow-up tasks.
steps: Generate 2 images in a Lara conversation
Scroll through the full conversation history
Inspect whether any raw image data (base64 strings) is visible
criteria: No raw base64 data (long strings of random characters) is visible in the chat
Images appear as rendered thumbnails or linked images — not text blobs
The conversation before and after image generation remains readable
Response time after image generation is not significantly slower (no context bloat)

title: Gmail Intelligence Suite
objective: Validate that the Gmail integration provides a powerful, AI-enhanced email management experience within the admin dashboard.

items.title: Gmail Account Connects Successfully
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to connect my Gmail account once and have it work reliably, so that I can manage my professional email from within my platform.
steps: Navigate to the Lara Email tab
Click "Connect Gmail"
Complete the Google OAuth authorization flow
Return to the email tab
criteria: The connect button is clearly visible and labeled
The Google authorization page appears correctly and the app's name/logo is shown
After authorizing, the user is redirected back to the email tab automatically
The connected Gmail address is displayed on the email tab
The inbox begins loading messages within 10 seconds

items.title: The Three-Panel Inbox Layout Is Intuitive
items.userStory: As a professional managing email within a web app, I want a familiar three-panel inbox experience so that I can read and manage emails efficiently.
steps: Open the Lara Email tab with Gmail connected
Browse the folder list (left panel)
Click different emails in the message list (middle panel)
Read email bodies in the reader pane (right panel)
criteria: Three panels are clearly visible: Folders | Message List | Reader Pane
Clicking an email in the list loads its full content in the reader pane within 2 seconds
HTML email bodies are rendered correctly and safely (no script execution)
The folder/label list shows standard folders (Inbox, Sent, Drafts, etc.)
Unread emails are visually distinguished from read emails

items.title: Email Actions Work (Star, Archive, Trash, Mark Read)
items.userStory: As an email user, I want to quickly perform standard email actions without leaving the inbox, so that I can keep my email organized.
steps: Star an email and verify it appears starred
Archive an email and verify it is removed from inbox
Trash an email and verify it is removed from inbox
Mark a read email as "unread"
criteria: All four actions work correctly on first attempt
After each action, the inbox list updates immediately (without full page reload)
Actions are reflected in the connected Gmail account (visible if Gmail is opened in another tab)
Each action has a clear visual button or icon that is easily identifiable

items.title: AI Helps Draft a New Email
items.userStory: As a busy professional, I want the AI to draft a professional email for me based on a brief description, so that I can write emails faster without sacrificing quality.
steps: Open the compose window
Use the "AI Compose" feature
Enter topic: "Request to extend the project deadline for the FAO climate resilience initiative by 2 weeks due to stakeholder feedback delays"
Click "Draft"
criteria: The AI generates a complete, professional email draft within 15 seconds
The draft includes: appropriate subject line, professional greeting, clear body, and sign-off
The tone is formal and appropriate for UN/FAO professional communication
The draft appears in the compose field and is fully editable
The draft does NOT contain any false facts or hallucinated project details

items.title: AI Polishes a Rough Draft
items.userStory: As a professional who writes quickly, I want the AI to refine my rough notes into a polished email, so that everything I send sounds professional.
steps: Type a rough draft in the compose window: "hi john, need the report by friday, im presenting next week and need the data. also can u remind the team about the meeting. thanks"
Click "Polish"
criteria: The AI produces a clearly improved version: proper greeting, professional tone, no abbreviations, complete sentences
The meaning of the original message is preserved — nothing is invented
The polished version appears in the compose field for review
The process completes within 15 seconds

items.title: AI Generates 3 Reply Suggestions for an Incoming Email
items.userStory: As a professional with a full inbox, I want the AI to suggest appropriate replies to incoming emails, so that I can respond quickly without spending time composing from scratch.
steps: Open any incoming email in the reader pane
Click "Reply Suggestions"
View the 3 suggested replies
criteria: 3 distinct reply suggestions are generated within 15 seconds
Each suggestion is a different approach: e.g. confirming, requesting more info, declining
Suggestions are appropriate to the email's content — not generic filler text
Clicking a suggestion populates the compose field with that reply (editable)
Suggestions are professional and match the expected communication style

items.title: AI Summarizes a Long Email
items.userStory: As a busy professional, I want to get a quick AI summary of a long email so that I can understand the key points without reading every paragraph.
steps: Open a long email (5+ paragraphs) in the reader pane
Click "Summarize"
Read the summary
criteria: A summary is generated within 10 seconds
The summary is 3-5 bullet points or a short paragraph
All key action items and important details from the email are captured
No information is fabricated — summary only reflects what's in the email
The summary is shown near the email, not replacing the original

items.title: Sending an Email Is Confirm-Guarded
items.userStory: As a professional, I want a confirmation step before sending any email, so that I never accidentally send an incomplete or wrong email.
steps: Compose an email (To, Subject, Body)
Click "Send"
Observe the confirmation dialog
Confirm the send
criteria: A confirmation dialog or guard appears before the email is sent
The dialog clearly asks "Are you sure you want to send this email?"
Clicking "Cancel" or "Go Back" returns to the compose window with the email intact
Clicking "Confirm Send" sends the email to the correct recipient
A success confirmation is shown after sending
The sent email appears in the Gmail Sent folder

items.title: The Floating Composer Is Draggable and Stackable
items.userStory: As a power user, I want to write emails while still seeing the inbox behind the compose window, so that I can reference other emails while composing.
steps: Open the compose window
Drag it by the title bar to a different position on the screen
Open a second compose window
criteria: The compose window can be dragged to any position on the screen
The inbox remains visible and interactive behind the dragged compose window
A second compose window can be opened simultaneously (stacked)
Both compose windows remain functional independently

title: Cross-Browser, Performance & Security
objective: Validate that the platform works reliably across browsers, loads quickly, and protects user data.

items.title: Site Loads Correctly in All Major Browsers
items.userStory: As a visitor using any browser, I want the site to look and work correctly, so that the experience is not dependent on which browser I happen to use.
steps: Open the public site in Google Chrome
Open the public site in Mozilla Firefox
Open the public site in Apple Safari
Open the public site in Microsoft Edge
criteria: The site loads without errors in all 4 browsers
The layout, fonts, and colors are consistent across all browsers
All interactive elements (globe, AI widget, network diagram) work in all browsers
No JavaScript errors appear in the browser console on any browser

items.title: Page Load Time Is Acceptable
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want the website to load quickly so that I am not kept waiting and do not leave out of frustration.
steps: Load the homepage on a standard broadband connection
Note the time to first meaningful content
Note the time to full interactive state
criteria: First meaningful content (text, hero section) appears within **3 seconds**
The page is fully interactive within **5 seconds**
The 3D globe, if present, loads without blocking the rest of the page
There is no visible "flash" of unstyled content

items.title: Admin Login Is Protected From Unauthorized Access
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to be confident that only I can access the admin dashboard, so that my content and platform settings are safe.
steps: Try accessing `/admin` without being logged in
Try accessing a private API endpoint (`/api/admin/projects`) without a session
Try entering the wrong password 5+ times in a row
criteria: Accessing `/admin` without a session shows only the login form — no admin content
The private API returns a 401 error without any content for unauthenticated requests
After multiple failed login attempts, access is temporarily blocked (rate limiting)
No admin data or structure is revealed to unauthenticated users

items.title: Sensitive Data Is Never Exposed Publicly
items.userStory: As the site owner, I want to be certain that no sensitive data (API keys, passwords, OAuth tokens) is ever exposed on the public website or in public API responses.
steps: View the source code of the public homepage
Call the public API (`/api/site-data`, `/api/projects`) and inspect the response
Open browser DevTools and check the Network tab for any leaks in public responses
criteria: No API keys, passwords, or secrets are visible in the page source
Public API responses contain only public content — no hashed passwords, OAuth tokens, or internal metadata
No admin URLs or structures are revealed in the public page source
The admin panel URL requires authentication — not publicly discoverable from page source

items.title: The Platform Handles Errors Gracefully
items.userStory: As a visitor, I want to see a helpful message if something goes wrong, rather than a confusing technical error page.
steps: Navigate to a non-existent page URL (e.g. `/project/this-does-not-exist`)
Disconnect internet and try to use the AI chat widget
Try submitting an empty form in admin
criteria: The 404 page is friendly and offers navigation back to the main site
The AI chat shows a user-friendly error message when offline
Empty form submissions show helpful validation messages (not crashes)
No stack traces, raw error objects, or server error pages are ever shown to users

title: Overall Platform Quality & Client Acceptance
objective: Final holistic review by the client to confirm the platform is launch-ready and accurately represents their professional identity.
personaFocus: Dr. Kavinda Gunasekara (Site Owner & Final Approver)

items.title: The Platform Accurately Represents Dr. Kavinda Gunasekara
items.userStory: As the platform owner, I want to confirm that every piece of content on the public site is accurate and represents me professionally, so that I am confident launching it to the world.
steps: Review the hero section — name, title, credentials
Review the About section — bio, stats
Browse all 10 projects — check for accuracy
Browse all 22 publications — check titles, authors, DOIs
Review the timeline milestones
criteria: My name is spelled correctly throughout the site
My professional title and organization (FAO) are correctly stated
All project titles and descriptions are accurate
All 22 publication titles, authors, journals, and years are correct
My citation count and h-index are accurate as of the launch date
The timeline milestones represent my actual career accurately
Nothing on the public site is embarrassing, inaccurate, or placeholder content

items.title: I Am Proud to Share This Website With Colleagues and Clients
items.userStory: As the platform owner, I want to feel genuinely proud of this website, confident that it meets or exceeds the quality standards of my international professional peers.
steps: View the full homepage as a first-time visitor would
Navigate to 3 random project detail pages
Try the floating AI widget from a visitor's perspective
View the site on a mobile device
criteria: The overall design feels premium and distinctive — not like a generic template
I would confidently send this URL to a UN Director, a FAO colleague, or an academic collaborator
The AI assistant impressed me with at least one accurate, helpful response
The mobile experience did not embarrass me in any way
The platform makes me look like the world-class expert that I am

items.title: I Can Manage the Platform Independently
items.userStory: As the platform owner, I want to be able to publish new content, update existing content, and manage my email — all without needing to call a developer.
steps: Publish a new draft blog post from the admin
Update the bio text in Settings
Generate a new image with Lara and apply it to a project
Reply to an email using an AI-generated draft
criteria: All four tasks were completed without needing any developer assistance
I did not need to write any code, access a database, or use a terminal
Each task took less than 5 minutes from start to finish
I feel confident that I could train another person to do these tasks from my team

items.title: The AI Super Agent Feels Like a Genuine Business Asset
items.userStory: As the platform owner, I want the Lara AI Super Agent to feel like a genuine business tool that saves me time — not a novelty feature.
steps: Delegate a real task to Lara (e.g. "Write a draft update for my project on climate-resilient agriculture and add it to the existing content")
Have Lara generate an image and apply it to that same project
Ask Lara to summarize what it did
criteria: The entire workflow (write → generate image → apply) completed without me writing a single line of text
The output quality was professional enough to use with minor editing
Lara's summary was accurate — it correctly described what it had done
I felt that Lara saved me at least 30 minutes of work
